name: No-Knead French Bread
recipeInstructions:
Combine warm water, sugar, and yeast in a small bowl; let stand for 5 minutes. Combine boiling water, sugar, butter, and salt in a large mixing bowl until butter melts. Add cold water; cool to lukewarm (105-115°F). Stir yeast mixture into water mixture. Gradually stir in flour until soft dough forms. Cover and rest for 10 minutes. Gently knead the dough a few times; cover again. Repeat gentle stirring every 10 minutes for the next 40 minutes.
Divide dough into three equal portions. Roll each portion into a 13 x 8-inch rectangle on a lightly floured surface. Roll up jellyroll style, starting with the long side; pinch ends and seam to seal. Place each loaf, seam side down, on a separate greased baking sheet. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free from drafts, about 40 minutes or until doubled in bulk. Make diagonal slits about 1/4-inch deep along the length of loaves using a sharp knife. Beat egg and milk together in a small bowl; gently brush over loaves after rising. Sprinkle each loaf with poppy seeds. Bake at 400°F for 20-25 minutes or until hollow when tapped. (This bread freezes well.) Yield: 3 loaves.
